note: Glossary config. Most groups resolve their terms from an existing source of truth via `source` (tools.json meta keys, or guides difficulties) so definitions live in one place. Only axes that have no description elsewhere (roles, subjects) carry inline `terms` here. This page is a placeholder home for these definitions — the longer-term intent is to surface each one as an inline tooltip where the label is used.
intro: Plain-language definitions for the labels and badges used across the site — the approval statuses on tools, and the categories used to filter and tag tools and guides.
placeholderNote: This is a temporary reference page. We expect most of these definitions to eventually appear as inline tooltips on the labels themselves, rather than living on a separate page.

groups.label: Approval status
groups.intro: A tool's review/approval status. Currently not shown publicly — tools are not independently reviewed yet; always confirm a tool's suitability and your school's policy before using it with students.

groups.label: Domain
groups.intro: The broad area of teaching work. Domains group the more specific 'work types' beneath them — choosing a domain narrows the work types you see.

groups.label: Work type
groups.intro: The job to be done, sitting under a domain. This is the primary way tools and guides are organised — start from the task, not the technology.

groups.label: Pedagogy
groups.intro: The teaching frameworks a tool or guide best supports. Each links through to a fuller explanation under Teaching.

groups.label: Year level
groups.intro: The schooling band a tool or guide is pitched at.

groups.label: Subject
groups.intro: Australian Curriculum learning areas. 'Cross-curricular' means a tool or guide isn't tied to one subject.

terms.def: Number, algebra, measurement, geometry, statistics and probability.
Biological, chemical, earth/space and physical sciences, plus science inquiry skills.
Humanities and Social Sciences — history, geography, civics and citizenship, and economics and business.
The Arts — visual arts, music, drama, dance and media arts.
Design and Technologies, and Digital Technologies.
Spans multiple learning areas rather than sitting in a single subject.

groups.label: Difficulty
groups.intro: How much prior AI experience a guide assumes.

groups.label: Availability & cost
groups.intro: How a tool is accessed — shown on tool detail pages. Cost and licensing details are best-guesses pending confirmation.
